Why Local-First Software Matters

By Elena Vasquez · January 15, 2024

The idea of local-first software is simple: your data lives on your device, and the cloud is optional. This is a radical departure from the SaaS model that dominates modern software.

The Problem with Cloud-Only

When your data lives exclusively in the cloud, you're dependent on the service provider for access. If they go down, you're stuck. If they change their pricing, you pay or lose access. If they shut down, your data may be gone forever.

What Local-First Means

Local-first doesn't mean offline-only. It means your device is the source of truth, and sync happens in the background when connectivity is available. The key principles are:

  • No spinners: The app works instantly because data is local.
  • Your data, your device: Data is stored on your machine first.
  • Network is optional: The app works fully offline.
  • Seamless collaboration: CRDTs enable conflict-free sync.

The Path Forward

Building local-first software is harder than building cloud-only software. But the result is software that respects users and stands the test of time.

Technologies like CRDTs, SQLite, and WebAssembly are making local-first development more practical than ever. The tools are ready — we just need to use them.
